mapping.a_element: D-brane as a localized/extended object that would classically shrink
mapping.b_element: discrete breather as a spatially localized lattice mode that can delocalize toward the band edge

mapping.a_element: Born-Infeld action giving the brane's energy/mass
mapping.b_element: Hamiltonian energy of the classical lattice degrees of freedom

mapping.a_element: existence of a stable finite-mass brane (no collapse to zero size)
mapping.b_element: positive lower bound on breather energy (energy cannot go to zero)

brief_justification: Both ask whether a localized object's energy can vanish, but the machinery differs: paper A stabilizes an extended brane against shrinking through a topological quantized U(1) worldvolume flux minimizing the Born-Infeld action (a discrete Myers-type stabilization matched to CFT), whereas paper B obtains a positive energy lower bound for discrete breathers from a scaling argument in the lattice dimension d relative to a critical dimension d_c. Flux quantization versus dimensional scaling of nonlinear lattice modes are distinct governing processes, so no role-for-role mechanism map holds.
